---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-18"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


## Packages

```{r echo=FALSE}
# ============================================================
# FIG 01 – Mean ES Values at Mining Sites (Faceted Bar)
# Each ES service gets its own panel with a free y-axis scale
# ============================================================
# Dependencies:
# install.packages(c("tidyverse", "sf", "scales", "ggtext", "patchwork"))
# ============================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(scales)
library(ggtext)   # rich text in titles/labels

ymd   <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y%m%d"); ymd

# ============================================================
# USER INPUTS
# ============================================================

BASE_DIR <- r"(C:\Users\yingjiel\Documents\mongolia-mining)"

# Output figure path
FIG_DIR <- file.path(BASE_DIR, 'figures', "es_footprint_figures")
dir.create(FIG_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
OUT_FIG <- file.path(FIG_DIR, "fig01_mean_bar_facet.png")
```


```{r}

# GeoPackage outputs from es_footprint_analysis.py
DATASETS <- list(
  # "Tang2023" = file.path(BASE_DIR, 'data', "es_footprint_outputs", "es_footprint_Tang2023.gpkg"),
  "Maus2022" = file.path(BASE_DIR, 'data', "es_footprint_outputs", "es_footprint_Maus2022.gpkg")
)

# Ecosystem service IDs (must match the es_id column in your ES table)
ES_IDS <- c(
  # "coastal_risk_reduction_service",
  "nitrogen_retention_service",
  "sediment_retention_service",
  "nature_access_service",
  "water_consumption" 
)

# Display labels for facet titles and axes
ES_LABELS <- c(
  # "coastal_risk_reduction_service" = "Coastal Risk Reduction",
  "nitrogen_retention_service"     = "Nitrogen Retention",
  "sediment_retention_service"     = "Sediment Retention",
  "nature_access_service"          = "Nature Access",
  "water_consumption"              = "Water Consumption"
)

# Dataset display labels
DS_LABELS <- c(
  # "Tang2023" = "Tang et al. 2023",
  "Maus2022" = "Maus et al. 2022"
)


```



## Load data

```{r}
# ============================================================
# LOAD & RESHAPE DATA
# ============================================================

load_col <- function(gpkg_path, 
                     dataset_label, 
                     column_postfix = '_mean' # or '_sum'
                       ) { 
  if (!file.exists(gpkg_path)) {
    message("  ✗ File not found, skipping: ", gpkg_path)
    return(NULL)
  }

  gdf <- st_read(gpkg_path, quiet = TRUE) |>
    st_drop_geometry()   # we only need the attribute table

  # pull out {es_id}_mean columns and pivot to long form
  selected_cols <- paste0(ES_IDS, column_postfix)
  available <- intersect(selected_cols, colnames(gdf))

  if (length(available) == 0) {
    message("  ✗ No ", column_postfix, " columns found in ", dataset_label)
    return(NULL)
  }

  gdf |>
    select(all_of(available)) |>
    pivot_longer(
      cols      = everything(),
      names_to  = "es_id",
      values_to = "value"
    ) |>
    mutate(
      es_id   = str_remove(es_id, paste0(column_postfix, "$")),
      dataset = dataset_label,
      metric = str_remove(column_postfix, "^_")
    )
}



library(purrr)

df_mean_list <- imap(DATASETS, ~ load_col(gpkg_path = .x,
                                          dataset_label = .y,
                                          column_postfix = "_mean"))

df_sum_list <- imap(DATASETS, ~ load_col(gpkg_path = .x,
                                         dataset_label = .y,
                                         column_postfix = "_sum"))

df_idx_list <- imap(DATASETS, ~ load_col(gpkg_path = .x,
                                         dataset_label = .y,
                                         column_postfix = "_adj_sum"))

df_mean <- list_rbind(df_mean_list)
df_sum  <- list_rbind(df_sum_list)
df_idx  <- list_rbind(df_idx_list)

```




```{r load water consumption}

## reload data
f <- file.path(BASE_DIR, "data", "water_consumption_cas.gpkg")
df_wat <- st_read(f)


names(df_wat)
names(df_sum)

df_wat_sum <- df_wat %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  mutate(
    es_id = 'water_consumption',
    value = annual_water_consumption_mm3 * 10^6, 
    dataset = 'CAS', 
    metric = 'sum'
  ) %>%
  select(es_id, value, dataset, metric)

```



```{r combine data}

df_sum_comb <- rbind(df_sum, df_wat_sum)

```




```{r clean label}


# attach display labels
df_mean_all <- df_mean |>
  mutate(
    es_label = ES_LABELS[es_id],
    ds_label = DS_LABELS[dataset],
    # keep factor order consistent with ES_IDS
    es_label = factor(es_label, levels = ES_LABELS[ES_IDS]),
    ds_label = factor(ds_label, levels = DS_LABELS)
  )


df_sum_all <- df_sum_comb |>
  filter(value > 0) %>%
  mutate(
    es_label = ES_LABELS[es_id],
    ds_label = DS_LABELS[dataset],
    # keep factor order consistent with ES_IDS
    es_label = factor(es_label, levels = ES_LABELS[ES_IDS]),
    ds_label = factor(ds_label, levels = DS_LABELS)
  ) %>%
  group_by(es_id) %>%
  mutate(total_sum = sum(value)) %>%
  filter(!es_id %in% c('nature_access_service')) %>%
  ungroup()


df_sum_label <- df_sum_all %>%
  group_by(es_id, es_label) %>%
  summarise(total_sum = sum(value), .groups = "drop")


```



```{r plot - sum}

library(scales)

n_services <- unique(df_sum_all$es_id) %>% length()
n_cols <- 2
n_rows <- floor(n_services/n_cols); n_rows

df_sum_all %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = value)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "#fe9929", color = "white") +
  # scale_y_log10(labels = scales::math_format(10^.x) ) +
  # scale_x_log10(labels = scales::math_format(10^.x) ) +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = trans_breaks("log10", function(x) 10^x),
                labels = trans_format("log10", math_format(10^.x))) +
  # scale_y_log10(breaks = trans_breaks("log10", function(x) 10^x),
  #               labels = trans_format("log10", math_format(10^.x))) +
  # annotation_logticks()  + 
  facet_wrap(~ es_label, scales = "free") +
  
  
  # add total sum text
  geom_text(
    data = df_sum_label,
    aes(
      x = Inf, 
      y = Inf, 
      # label = paste0("Total = ", scales::comma(total_sum)),
      label = paste0("Total = ", scales::label_number(
        scale_cut = scales::cut_short_scale(), 
        accuracy = 0.1
        )(total_sum))
    ),
    hjust = 1.1, # 1.1
    vjust = 1.5,
    size = 3.5, 
    fontface="italic",
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
    
    
  labs(
    x = "Total",
    y = "Count",
    # title = "Distribution of Sum Values"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)


out_c <- file.path(FIG_DIR, paste0("figA_es_sum_hist_", ymd, ".png"))
ggsave(out_c, plot = last_plot(), width = 7, height = 2*n_rows, dpi = 600, bg = "transparent")
```




## Stats + Plot 

```{r}
# ============================================================
# SUMMARY STATS (mean ± se per ES × dataset)
# ============================================================

df_summary <- df_mean_all |>
  group_by(es_label, ds_label) |>
  summarise(
    mean_val = mean(value,  na.rm = TRUE),
    se       = sd(value,   na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(value))),
    n        = sum(!is.na(value)),
    .groups  = "drop"
  )

message("Summary stats computed for ", nrow(df_summary), " ES × dataset combinations")


# ============================================================
# PLOT
# ============================================================

# dataset colours
ds_colours <- c(
  # "Tang et al. 2023" = "#1f77b4",
  "Maus et al. 2022" = "#ff7f0e"
)
# fall back gracefully if only one dataset is present
ds_colours <- ds_colours[names(ds_colours) %in% levels(df_summary$ds_label)]

p <- ggplot(df_summary,
            aes(x     = ds_label,
                y     = mean_val,
                fill  = ds_label,
                label = number(mean_val, accuracy = 0.01, big.mark = ","))) +

  # bars
  geom_col(width = 0.6, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +

  # error bars (± 1 SE)
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_val - se,
                    ymax = mean_val + se),
                width = 0.18, linewidth = 0.6, colour = "grey30") +

  # value labels above bars
  geom_text(aes(y = mean_val + se),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 3, colour = "grey20") +

  # one panel per ES, each with its own y scale
  facet_wrap(~ es_label,
             scales = "free_y",
             ncol   = 2) +

  scale_fill_manual(values = ds_colours, name = NULL) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = label_number(big.mark = ",", accuracy = 0.01),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18))   # headroom above bars for labels
  ) +

  labs(
    title    = "Mean Ecosystem Service Values at Mining Sites",
    subtitle = "Error bars = ±1 SE across polygons  |  y-axis free per panel",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Mean Value",
    caption  = "Sources: Tang et al. 2023; Maus et al. 2022"
  ) +

  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(colour = "grey40", size = 10),
    plot.caption     = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 9, hjust = 1),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "#f0f0f0", colour = NA),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title.y     = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    legend.key.size  = unit(0.4, "cm"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank()
  )


# ============================================================
# SAVE
# ============================================================

ggsave(
  filename = OUT_FIG,
  plot     = p,
  width    = 10,
  height   = 8,
  dpi      = 300,
  bg       = "white"
)

message("✓ Figure saved: ", OUT_FIG)
print(p)


```


## Map 
```{r}
# ============================================================
# SPATIAL POINT MAPS – ES Mean & Adj-Sum at Mining Sites
# ============================================================
# Converts mining polygons to centroids so each site appears
# as a single point regardless of polygon size.
# Produces two figure sets:
#   Fig A – Mean value maps  (one facet per ES service)
#   Fig B – Adj-sum maps     (one facet per ES service)
#   Fig C – Combined 2-row dashboard (mean | adj-sum)
#
# Dependencies:
#   install.packages(c("tidyverse", "sf", "scales",
#                      "ggspatial", "rnaturalearth",
#                      "rnaturalearthdata", "patchwork"))
# ============================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(scales)
library(ggspatial)          # north arrow + scale bar
library(rnaturalearth)      # country basemap
library(rnaturalearthdata)  # required by rnaturalearth
library(patchwork)          # stitch panels together

# ============================================================
# USER INPUTS
# ============================================================



# Point appearance
POINT_SIZE   <- 1.8    # base point size
POINT_ALPHA  <- 0.85   # transparency
POINT_STROKE <- 0.1    # outline thickness

# Colour palettes – one per stat type
PALETTE_MEAN <- "YlOrRd"   # sequential: low → high mean value
PALETTE_SUM  <- "PuBuGn"   # sequential: low → high adj-sum

# Basemap country fill / border
COUNTRY_FILL   <- "#f5f5f0"
COUNTRY_BORDER <- "#c8c8c8"

# ============================================================
# HELPERS
# ============================================================

#' Load a gpkg, drop geometry, reproject to WGS84, return centroid sf
load_centroids <- function(gpkg_path, dataset_label) {
  if (!file.exists(gpkg_path)) {
    stop("File not found: ", gpkg_path)
  }
  message("  Loading: ", gpkg_path)

  gdf <- st_read(gpkg_path, quiet = TRUE) |>
    st_make_valid() |>
    st_transform(4326)   # ensure WGS84 for plotting

  # centroid of each polygon
  centroids <- gdf |>
    mutate(geometry = st_centroid(st_geometry(gdf))) |>
    mutate(dataset = dataset_label)

  message("  ✓ ", nrow(centroids), " centroids  |  CRS: EPSG:4326")
  centroids
}


#' Pivot one stat (mean or adj_sum) to long form, attach ES labels
pivot_stat <- function(sf_obj, stat_suffix) {
  cols    <- paste0(ES_IDS, stat_suffix)
  present <- intersect(cols, colnames(sf_obj))

  if (length(present) == 0) {
    stop("No columns matching *", stat_suffix, " found.")
  }

  sf_obj |>
    select(dataset, all_of(present), geometry) |>
    pivot_longer(
      cols      = all_of(present),
      names_to  = "es_id",
      values_to = "value"
    ) |>
    mutate(
      es_id    = str_remove(es_id, paste0(stat_suffix, "$")),
      es_label = factor(ES_LABELS[es_id], levels = ES_LABELS[ES_IDS])
    )
}


#' Winsorise to [p_low, p_high] quantiles to reduce outlier distortion
winsorise <- function(x, p_low = 0.02, p_high = 0.98) {
  qs <- quantile(x, c(p_low, p_high), na.rm = TRUE)
  pmax(pmin(x, qs[2]), qs[1])
}


#' World basemap cropped to the bounding box of an sf object (+buffer_deg)
basemap <- function(sf_obj, buffer_deg = 0.5) {
  bb <- st_bbox(sf_obj)
  world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")
  st_crop(world,
          xmin = as.numeric(bb["xmin"]) - buffer_deg,
          xmax = as.numeric(bb["xmax"]) + buffer_deg,
          ymin = as.numeric(bb["ymin"]) - buffer_deg,
          ymax = as.numeric(bb["ymax"]) + buffer_deg)
}


#' Build one faceted point map
#'   long_sf  : long-form sf with columns value, es_label, geometry
#'   stat_label: "Mean Value" or "Area-Adjusted Sum"
#'   palette   : viridis/brewer palette name
#'   ds_label  : dataset display string
make_facet_map <- function(long_sf, stat_label, palette, ds_label) {

  # winsorise per ES panel to avoid a single outlier swamping the scale
  long_sf <- long_sf |>
    group_by(es_label) |>
    mutate(value_w = winsorise(value)) |>
    ungroup()

  world_crop <- basemap(long_sf, buffer_deg = 1.5)

  ggplot() +

    # ── basemap ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    geom_sf(data  = world_crop,
            fill  = COUNTRY_FILL,
            color = COUNTRY_BORDER,
            linewidth = 0.25) +

    # ── mining-site points ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    geom_sf(data  = long_sf,
            aes(colour = value_w),
            size   = POINT_SIZE,
            alpha  = POINT_ALPHA,
            stroke = POINT_STROKE,
            shape  = 16) +

    # ── colour scale (continuous, winsorised) ─────────────────────────────────
    scale_colour_distiller(
      palette  = palette,
      direction = 1,
      name     = stat_label,
      labels   = label_number(big.mark = ",", accuracy = 0.01),
      guide    = guide_colourbar(
        title.position = "top",
        title.hjust    = 0.5,
        barwidth       = unit(6, "cm"),
        barheight      = unit(0.4, "cm"),
        ticks.linewidth = 0.5
      )
    ) +

    # ── one panel per ES, each with own colour scale ──────────────────────────
    facet_wrap(~ es_label, ncol = 2) + # , scales = "free"

    # ── cartographic extras ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
    annotation_scale(location = "bl", width_hint = 0.25,
                     text_cex = 0.6, line_width = 0.5) +
    annotation_north_arrow(location = "tr",
                           style    = north_arrow_minimal(text_size = 7),
                           height   = unit(0.6, "cm"),
                           width    = unit(0.6, "cm")) +

    coord_sf(expand = FALSE) +

    labs(
      title    = paste0(stat_label, " of Ecosystem Services at Mining Sites"),
      subtitle = paste0(ds_label,
                        "  |  Points = polygon centroids  |  ",
                        "Colour winsorised to 2nd–98th percentile per panel"),
      caption  = "Basemap: Natural Earth  |  CRS: WGS84"
    ) +

    theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      plot.title        = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
      plot.subtitle     = element_text(colour = "grey40", size = 9),
      plot.caption      = element_text(colour = "grey55", size = 8, hjust = 1),
      strip.text        = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
      strip.background  = element_rect(fill = "#eeeeee", colour = NA),
      legend.position   = "bottom",
      legend.title      = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"),
      legend.text       = element_text(size = 8),
      axis.text         = element_text(size = 7),
      axis.title        = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major  = element_line(colour = "#e8e8e8", linewidth = 0.3),
      panel.spacing     = unit(0.6, "lines")
    )
}


#' Build a 2-row combined dashboard (mean top, adj_sum bottom)
#'   for a single dataset
make_dashboard <- function(long_mean, long_sum, ds_label) {
  p_mean <- make_facet_map(long_mean, "Mean Value",
                           PALETTE_MEAN, ds_label) +
    theme(plot.title    = element_blank(),
          plot.subtitle = element_blank(),
          plot.caption  = element_blank())

  p_sum  <- make_facet_map(long_sum,  "Area-Adjusted Sum",
                           PALETTE_SUM,  ds_label) +
    theme(plot.title    = element_blank(),
          plot.subtitle = element_blank())

  p_mean / p_sum +
    plot_annotation(
      title    = paste0("Ecosystem Services at Mining Sites – ", ds_label),
      subtitle = "Top: Mean value per polygon centroid  |  Bottom: Area-adjusted sum",
      caption  = "Basemap: Natural Earth  |  Points = polygon centroids  |  CRS: WGS84",
      theme    = theme(
        plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey40", size = 10),
        plot.caption  = element_text(colour = "grey55", size = 8, hjust = 1)
      )
    )
}


# ============================================================
# MAIN
# ============================================================

message("\n", strrep("=", 65))
message("SPATIAL POINT MAPS – ES MEAN & ADJ-SUM")
message(strrep("=", 65), "\n")

for (label in names(DATASETS)) {
  message("── Dataset: ", label, " ──────────────────────────────────")

  centroids <- tryCatch(
    load_centroids(DATASETS[[label]], label),
    error = function(e) { message("  ✗ ", e$message); NULL }
  )
  if (is.null(centroids)) next

  ds_label   <- DS_LABELS[label]
  long_mean  <- pivot_stat(centroids, "_mean")
  long_sum   <- pivot_stat(centroids, "_adj_sum")

  # ── Fig A: mean facet map ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
  message("  Plotting Fig A – Mean value map...")
  p_a <- make_facet_map(long_mean, "Mean Value", PALETTE_MEAN, ds_label)
  out_a <- file.path(FIG_DIR, paste0("figA_spatial_mean_", label, ".png"))
  ggsave(out_a, p_a, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
  message("  ✓ Saved: ", basename(out_a))

  # ── Fig B: adj-sum facet map ───────────────────────────────────────────────
  message("  Plotting Fig B – Adj-sum map...")
  p_b <- make_facet_map(long_sum, "Area-Adjusted Sum", PALETTE_SUM, ds_label)
  out_b <- file.path(FIG_DIR, paste0("figB_spatial_adjsum_", label, ".png"))
  ggsave(out_b, p_b, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
  message("  ✓ Saved: ", basename(out_b))

  # ── Fig C: combined dashboard ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  message("  Plotting Fig C – Combined dashboard...")
  p_c <- make_dashboard(long_mean, long_sum, ds_label)
  out_c <- file.path(FIG_DIR, paste0("figC_spatial_dashboard_", label, ".png"))
  ggsave(out_c, p_c, width = 12, height = 18, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
  message("  ✓ Saved: ", basename(out_c))

  message("")
}

message(strrep("=", 65))
message("✓ All figures saved to: ", FIG_DIR)
message(strrep("=", 65))

```

## Map - point size

```{r}

# ── Bubble size range (ggplot units) ──────────────────────────────────────────
# Increase MAX_BUBBLE if sites are sparse; decrease if they overlap too much
MIN_BUBBLE <- 0.5
MAX_BUBBLE <- 6

# ── Point transparency ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
POINT_ALPHA <- 0.75

# ── Colour palette for polygon AREA (the 2nd variable) ───────────────────────
# Uses a diverging-ish sequential palette so small vs large mines stand out
AREA_PALETTE   <- "Blues"    # darker = larger mine polygon
AREA_PALETTE_D <- 1          # direction: 1 = light→dark with increasing value

# ── Basemap ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
COUNTRY_FILL   <- "#f0ede8"
COUNTRY_BORDER <- "#c0bbb4"


# ============================================================
# HELPERS
# ============================================================

#' Load gpkg → validate → reproject → compute centroids + polygon area (km²)
load_centroids <- function(gpkg_path, dataset_label) {
  if (!file.exists(gpkg_path)) stop("File not found: ", gpkg_path)
  message("  Loading: ", gpkg_path)

  gdf <- st_read(gpkg_path, quiet = TRUE) |>
    st_make_valid() |>
    st_transform(4326)

  # area in km² (reproject to equal-area for accurate measurement)
  area_m2 <- gdf |>
    st_transform(6933) |>   # WGS 84 / NSIDC EASE-Grid 2.0 (equal-area)
    st_area() |>
    as.numeric()

  centroids <- gdf |>
    mutate(
      polygon_area_km2 = area_m2 / 1e6,
      geometry         = st_centroid(st_geometry(gdf)),
      dataset          = dataset_label
    )

  message("  ✓ ", nrow(centroids), " centroids  |  ",
          "area range: ", round(min(area_m2 / 1e6), 2), "–",
          round(max(area_m2 / 1e6), 1), " km²")
  centroids
}


#' Pivot one stat suffix (_mean or _adj_sum) to long form
#' Carries polygon_area_km2 along for colour encoding
pivot_stat <- function(sf_obj, stat_suffix) {
  cols    <- paste0(ES_IDS, stat_suffix)
  present <- intersect(cols, colnames(sf_obj))
  if (length(present) == 0) stop("No columns matching *", stat_suffix)

  sf_obj |>
    select(dataset, polygon_area_km2, all_of(present), geometry) |>
    pivot_longer(
      cols      = all_of(present),
      names_to  = "es_id",
      values_to = "value"
    ) |>
    mutate(
      es_id    = str_remove(es_id, paste0(stat_suffix, "$")),
      es_label = factor(ES_LABELS[es_id], levels = ES_LABELS[ES_IDS])
    )
}


#' Winsorise to [p_low, p_high] — applied per ES panel
winsorise <- function(x, p_low = 0.02, p_high = 0.98) {
  qs <- quantile(x, c(p_low, p_high), na.rm = TRUE)
  pmax(pmin(x, qs[2]), qs[1])
}


#' Crop world basemap to sf bounding box + buffer
basemap_crop <- function(sf_obj, buffer_deg = 1.5) {
  bb    <- st_bbox(sf_obj)
  world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")
  st_crop(world,
          xmin = as.numeric(bb["xmin"]) - buffer_deg, xmax = as.numeric(bb["xmax"]) + buffer_deg,
          ymin = as.numeric(bb["ymin"]) - buffer_deg, ymax = as.numeric(bb["ymax"]) + buffer_deg)
}


#' Core bubble map function
#'
#' SIZE   = ES value (winsorised per panel, free scale across facets)
#' COLOUR = log10(polygon_area_km2) — constant across all panels
#'
#' @param long_sf     long-form sf with columns: value, es_label,
#'                    polygon_area_km2, geometry
#' @param stat_label  axis / legend title string
#' @param ds_label    dataset display name for subtitle
make_bubble_map <- function(long_sf, stat_label, ds_label) {

  # winsorise size variable per panel so one huge outlier
  # doesn't miniaturise every other point
  long_sf <- long_sf |>
    group_by(es_label) |>
    mutate(value_w = winsorise(value)) |>
    ungroup() |>
    filter(!is.na(value_w), !is.na(polygon_area_km2))

  world_crop <- basemap_crop(long_sf)

  # shared colour limits across all panels (log scale)
  area_log     <- log10(long_sf$polygon_area_km2 + 1e-3)
  area_log_lim <- quantile(area_log, c(0.01, 0.99), na.rm = TRUE)

  ggplot() +

    # ── country basemap ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    geom_sf(data = world_crop,
            fill = COUNTRY_FILL, colour = COUNTRY_BORDER,
            linewidth = 0.25) +

    # ── bubbles: size = ES value, colour = polygon area ───────────────────────
    geom_sf(data  = long_sf |> arrange(value_w),   # plot small bubbles last
            aes(size   = value_w,
                colour = log10(polygon_area_km2 + 1e-3)),
            alpha  = POINT_ALPHA,
            shape  = 16) +

    # ── size scale: free per facet via after_scale isn't possible in facet_wrap
    #    so we use scale_size_area (area ∝ value — perceptually correct)
    scale_size_area(
      name     = stat_label,
      max_size = MAX_BUBBLE,
      labels   = label_number(big.mark = ",", accuracy = 0.1),
      guide    = guide_legend(
        title.position = "top",
        title.hjust    = 0.5,
        override.aes   = list(alpha = 0.9, colour = "grey40"),
        nrow           = 1
      )
    ) +

    # ── colour scale: polygon area (log₁₀ km²) ───────────────────────────────
    scale_colour_distiller(
      palette   = AREA_PALETTE,
      direction = AREA_PALETTE_D,
      limits    = area_log_lim,
      oob       = squish,
      name      = "Mine area (km²)",
      breaks    = log10(c(0.01, 0.1, 1, 10, 100, 1000)),
      labels    = c("0.01", "0.1", "1", "10", "100", "1 000"),
      guide     = guide_colourbar(
        title.position  = "top",
        title.hjust     = 0.5,
        barwidth        = unit(5, "cm"),
        barheight       = unit(0.4, "cm"),
        ticks.linewidth = 0.5
      )
    ) +

    # ── free size scale per panel (same geographic extent) ───────────────────
    facet_wrap(~ es_label, ncol = 2) + # , scales = "free"

    # ── cartographic annotations ──────────────────────────────────────────────
    annotation_scale(location    = "bl", width_hint = 0.22,
                     text_cex    = 0.55, line_width = 0.4) +
    annotation_north_arrow(location = "tr",
                           style    = north_arrow_minimal(text_size = 6),
                           height   = unit(0.55, "cm"),
                           width    = unit(0.55, "cm")) +

    coord_sf(expand = FALSE) +

    labs(
      title    = paste0(stat_label, " at Mining Sites"),
      subtitle = paste0(
        ds_label,
        "  |  Bubble size = ", tolower(stat_label),
        " (winsorised 2–98th pct per panel)",
        "  |  Colour = mine polygon area"
      ),
      caption = "Basemap: Natural Earth  |  Area computed in equal-area projection  |  CRS: WGS84"
    ) +

    theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(colour = "grey35", size = 8.5),
      plot.caption     = element_text(colour = "grey55", size = 7.5, hjust = 1),
      strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "#e8e8e8", colour = NA),
      legend.position  = "bottom",
      legend.box       = "horizontal",
      legend.title     = element_text(size = 8.5, face = "bold"),
      legend.text      = element_text(size = 7.5),
      legend.spacing.x = unit(0.8, "cm"),
      axis.text        = element_text(size = 6.5),
      axis.title       = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "#e4e4e4", linewidth = 0.25),
      panel.spacing    = unit(0.5, "lines")
    )
}


#' Stacked dashboard: mean (top) + adj-sum (bottom)
make_dashboard <- function(long_mean, long_sum, ds_label) {

  strip <- function(p)
    p + theme(plot.title    = element_blank(),
              plot.subtitle = element_blank(),
              plot.caption  = element_blank())

  strip(make_bubble_map(long_mean, "Mean ES Value",         ds_label)) /
  strip(make_bubble_map(long_sum,  "Area-Adjusted ES Sum",  ds_label)) +
    plot_annotation(
      title    = paste0("Ecosystem Services Bubble Maps  –  ", ds_label),
      subtitle = paste0(
        "Top: Mean value per centroid  |  Bottom: Area-adjusted sum  |  ",
        "Bubble size = ES value  |  Colour = mine area (km²)"
      ),
      caption  = "Basemap: Natural Earth  |  CRS: WGS84",
      theme    = theme(
        plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(colour = "grey35", size = 9),
        plot.caption  = element_text(colour = "grey55", size = 8, hjust = 1)
      )
    )
}


# ============================================================
# MAIN
# ============================================================

message("\n", strrep("=", 65))
message("SPATIAL BUBBLE MAPS – ES MEAN & ADJ-SUM")
message("  SIZE = ES value  |  COLOUR = mine polygon area")
message(strrep("=", 65), "\n")

for (label in names(DATASETS)) {
  message("── Dataset: ", label, " ──────────────────────────────────")

  centroids <- tryCatch(
    load_centroids(DATASETS[[label]], label),
    error = function(e) { message("  ✗ ", e$message); NULL }
  )
  if (is.null(centroids)) next

  ds_label  <- DS_LABELS[label]
  long_mean <- pivot_stat(centroids, "_mean")
  long_sum  <- pivot_stat(centroids, "_adj_sum")

  # ── Fig A: mean bubble map ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  message("  Plotting Fig A – Mean bubble map...")
  p_a   <- make_bubble_map(long_mean, "Mean ES Value", ds_label)
  out_a <- file.path(FIG_DIR, paste0("figA_bubble_mean_", label, ".png"))
  ggsave(out_a, p_a, width = 13, height = 11, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
  message("  ✓ Saved: ", basename(out_a))

  # ── Fig B: adj-sum bubble map ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  message("  Plotting Fig B – Adj-sum bubble map...")
  p_b   <- make_bubble_map(long_sum, "Area-Adjusted ES Sum", ds_label)
  out_b <- file.path(FIG_DIR, paste0("figB_bubble_adjsum_", label, ".png"))
  ggsave(out_b, p_b, width = 13, height = 11, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
  message("  ✓ Saved: ", basename(out_b))

  # ── Fig C: stacked dashboard ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  message("  Plotting Fig C – Combined dashboard...")
  p_c   <- make_dashboard(long_mean, long_sum, ds_label)
  out_c <- file.path(FIG_DIR, paste0("figC_bubble_dashboard_", label, ".png"))
  ggsave(out_c, p_c, width = 13, height = 20, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
  message("  ✓ Saved: ", basename(out_c))

  message("")
}
```
